title: dianthus pungens subsp. insignitus
intro: Dianthus pungens subsp. insignitus, also known as the rock pink or mountain pink, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dianthus pungens subsp. insignitus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
